name: Ise Grand Shrine
shortDescription: Ise Grand Shrine is the most sacred Shinto shrine in Japan, dedicated to the sun goddess Amaterasu-ōmikami. It consists of two main shrines, Naiku (Inner Shrine) dedicated to Amaterasu and Geku (Outer Shrine) dedicated to Toyouke-ōmikami, the deity of food, clothing, and housing.
description: Ise Grand Shrine (伊勢神宮, Ise Jingū), located in Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan, is a Shinto shrine complex dedicated to the goddess Amaterasu-ōmikami, the sun goddess and one of the most important deities in the Shinto pantheon. It is considered the most sacred Shinto shrine in Japan and a place of profound spiritual significance. The shrine is not dedicated to a single deity but is a complex of over 125 shrines, centered on the two main shrines: Naiku (Inner Shrine), dedicated to Amaterasu, and Geku (Outer Shrine), dedicated to Toyouke-ōmikami, the deity of agriculture, industry, and food. The Naiku enshrines the Sacred Mirror, Yata-no-Kagami, one of the Three Sacred Treasures of Japan, said to have been used to lure Amaterasu out of a cave, restoring light to the world. The Geku provides Amaterasu with food and clothing. The architecture is characterized by its simplicity and use of natural materials, primarily Japanese cypress (hinoki). The structures are rebuilt every 20 years in a ritual called Shikinen Sengu, maintaining the ancient building techniques and traditions. This ritual symbolizes renewal and the cyclical nature of life and death. The shrine's origins are shrouded in myth and legend, with its founding traditionally attributed to the legendary Empress Yamatohime-no-mikoto in the 3rd century CE. The shrine has been a center of imperial patronage and pilgrimage for centuries and remains a vital part of Japanese culture and religious identity. Access to the inner sanctuaries is restricted to priests and members of the Imperial Family, adding to its mystique and sacredness. The Ise Grand Shrine exemplifies the close relationship between Shinto beliefs, nature, and Japanese identity.

location.cosmological: Located in Ise, Mie Prefecture, Japan, considered a sacred space on Earth connected to the celestial realm.
